mission.title: spire: Provide the ability to disable JWT-SVID support in SPIRE
mission.description: Provide the ability to disable JWT-SVID support in SPIRE. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current spire deployment
steps.description:
Verify your spire version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
helm list -n spire 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working spire installation.

steps.title: Review spire configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get configmap -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=spire
```
There are some scenarios where JWT-SVID support is not needed, and servers could avoid having to maintain JWT authorities and the rotation of JWT keys.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Provide the ability to disable JWT-SVID support in SPIRE
steps.description:
Spire server JWT-SVID

New option to disable JWT-SVID in spire server. JWT keys are no longer managed. Server and Agent api endpoints related to JWT return precondition failed grpc code

Answers from the issue:
- returning FailedPrecondition grpc code in JWT related endpoints

- decided to go with
```yaml
time="2025-08-26T13:35:22Z" level=error msg="JWT functionality is disabled" authorized_as=local authorized_via=transport method=MintJWTSVID request_id=5f535de6-d5e5-466a-beb4-ceab4f32e88f service=svid.v1.SVID subsystem_name=api
time="2025-08-26T13:35:22Z" level=error msg="JWT functionality is disabled" authorized_as=local authorized_via=transport method=PrepareJWTAuthority request_id=a756696c-c986-4e10-8f41-7c10a769f9e3 service=localauthority.v1.LocalAuthority subsystem_name=api
time="2025-08-26T13:35:23Z" level=error msg="JWT functionality is disabled" authorized_as=local authorized_via=trans
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get events -n spire --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Provide the ability to disable JWT-SVID support in SPIRE" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Spire server JWT-SVID

New option to disable JWT-SVID in spire server. JWT keys are no longer managed.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
